mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
options: A. An object at rest stays at rest.
B. Force equals mass times acceleration.
C. For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
D. An object moves in a straight line unless acted upon.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of Newton's First Law?
options: A. A car accelerating on a highway.
B. A book resting on a table.
C. A ball being thrown.
D. A satellite orbiting Earth.

mcqs.question: Inertia is defined as the tendency of an object to:
options: A. Change its position.
B. Stay at rest or in uniform motion.
C. Accelerate.
D. Apply force.

mcqs.question: What is the effect of mass on inertia?
options: A. More mass means less inertia.
B. More mass means more inertia.
C. Mass has no effect on inertia.
D. Inertia is constant regardless of mass.

mcqs.question: Which object has more inertia?
options: A. A feather.
B. A soccer ball.
C. A car.
D. A bicycle.

fill_in_the_blanks.question: According to Newton's First Law, an object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by a __________.
The property of matter that causes it to resist changes in its state of motion is called __________.
Objects with greater __________ have greater inertia.
If no net force acts on an object, its velocity will remain __________.
Newton's First Law is often referred to as the law of __________.

short_questions.question: What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
Provide an example of Newton's First Law in everyday life.
What happens to a ball thrown in space?
